sections.subject: Physical Education

entries.title: About the Course
entries.content:
This course includes the following topic(s): Playground Songs & Rhymes, Jump Rope, Free Play, Folk Dancing

About Playground Songs & Rhymes
Develop hand-eye coordination while playing with words and rhythm using ball-bouncing, passing, and clapping games.

About Jump Rope
Using jump-rope games, students work on hand-eye coordination while playing with words and rhythm.

About Folk Dancing
Learn basic steps for individual, couple, and/or group dances using traditional folk songs.

PE: Grades 1-3
Lessons specifically created for Grades 1-6. We recommend you make sure to include some sort of physical activity as breaks throughout your school day. If you already have sports or similar activities as a regular part of your life rhythms, these lessons are optional.

entries.content:
Permission to print for non-commercial use. See Alveary group use policy to use lessons
in a group context.

LINKS: Click text or scan the QR code in the top corner of the lesson plan pages to view
online resources associated with the lessons.

Responsibility for previewing all links rests with the teacher. All links were checked at the
time of publication; however, websites change frequently and may contain objectionable
content. Please report broken links by contacting us through our website.

PE: Grades 1-3
☐ Read the Introduction for Playground Songs & Rhymes p.5

☐ Bookmark the song playlist on SoundCloud. These include songs for folk dances, playground songs and rhymes, and jump rope. Create a free SoundCloud account to use offline. There are links to individual songs in lessons.

☐ Preview games and songs.

Folk Dancing
☐ Read the Introduction for The Book of Song Dances p.4-9.

☐ Bookmark the video folder of folk dances.

☐ Preview dance video; learn beforehand to teach students.

supplies.title: Long Jump Rope and Short Jump Rope

blocks.text: Mason emphasized that being physically fit is just as important as being mentally and spiritually fit. She used a combination of Swedish Drill (calisthenic exercises done to music or military-style commands), playground games, sports, dance, free play, and afternoon walks to make sure students had time for vigorous exercise every single day.
Teach students the new song, dance, or sport. Tutorials are often provided.
Play your new game, practice your new dance, sing your new rhyme, etc.!

terms.content:
Playground Songs & Rhymes
·  Perform "Hot Cross Buns" OR "Where, Oh, Where" for the teacher to observe.

Folk Dancing
· Perform the song and dance for Great Big House OR Brother Come and Dance.

Playground Songs & Rhymes
· Perform "Say, Say, My Playmate" OR "Obwisana" for the teacher to observe.

Folk Dancing
· Perform the song and dance for Old Joe Clark OR Brown Eyed Mary.

Playground Songs & Rhymes
· Perform "My Momma Gave Me a Nickel" OR "I Pass the Shoe" for the teacher to observe.

Folk Dancing
· Perform the song and dance for Billy, Billy OR Paw Paw Patch.
